results.title: LLM Stats: Compare API models by benchmarks, cost & capabilities
results.content:
Now, we're working towards building the best semi-private, open and reproducible AI benchmarking community. We believe there's a greater need for independent benchmarks and environments that measure the progress of AI in areas like coding, science, visuals and long horizon tasks.

We're backed by Y Combinator and leaders of Hugging Face, Harvard Medical School, Daytona, Insight Data Science and many more.

Would love to hear your thoughts, see you on the platform.

Chris Hicken
Theysaid

Theysaid

@jonathanchavez This is exactly what the AI community needs right now! How do you plan to keep the benchmarks up-to-date as new models are released? It’d be great to see how you handle emerging models over time.

Jonathan Chávez
LLM Stats

LLM Stats

@chrishicken

Thanks Chris! [...] Lilou Lane

Really impressed by the vision here. Independent, reproducible benchmarks are critical for meaningful comparisons, and building a semi-private community adds a lot of trust. Looking forward to exploring the platform. [...] LLM Stats

results.title: Foundation Models Reviewed & Ranked for 2026 - Product Hunt
results.content:
## Top reviewed foundation models

Top reviewed

Across the most-reviewed foundation models, the market splits between broad API platforms, open and portable stacks, and specialist reasoning or multimodal options. OpenAI stands out for end-to-end app building with tools, memory, voice, vision, and agents; Mistral AI emphasizes privacy, self-hosting, and multilingual enterprise workflows; DeepSeek competes on affordable coding, research, and long-document analysis."

Wispr Flow: Dictation That Works Everywhere [...] Key features:

 Autonomous long-horizon research workflows
 MCP support for custom data integration
 Native visualizations (charts & infographics)
 Multimodal inputs (PDFs, CSVs, media)
 Real-time reasoning + collaborative planning

Perfect for analysts, enterprises, and teams in finance, life sciences, and market research who need fast, reliable, and deep insights.

If you're building or scaling with AI agents, this is worth exploring!

results.title: Z.ai: Official Playground for High-Performance GLM Models
results.content:
Flowtica Scribe

Hi everyone!  
  
Have been using ZCode since its first version late last year. With the recent 3.0 upgrade, it's taken a massive leap forward. Here are a few reasons it’s worth a try:  
  
It’s the official harness for GLM-5.2. Yes, try the tuning that understands the true upper bound of GLM-5.2 better than anything else!  
  
It’s built for long-horizon execution. The in-house ZCode Agent keeps your files, terminal output, browser context, and Git state stitched together in the same task loop, so it doesn't lose the thread halfway through a complex build.  
  
It lets you step away from the desk. You can steer the agent, check progress, and kick off tasks from your phone, or directly through messaging bots while the desktop keeps running.  
  
And it’s moving fast! [...] Z.ai

# Z.ai

## Official Playground for High-Performance GLM Models

1.5K followers

Rohan Chaubey [...] 77.6% on SWE-Bench Verified

Open weights on Hugging Face under a modified MIT license

Self-hostable on 4 GPUs

API at $1.5/M input tokens and $7.5/M output tokens

Powers Vibe remote coding agents and Le Chat Work mode (Pro/Team/Enterprise plans)

Available on NVIDIA build.nvidia.com and as an NIM container

Benefits:

Run a frontier-class model on your own infrastructure without a large GPU cluster

Tune reasoning depth at the API level, useful for cost-sensitive agentic pipelines

Single model handles the full range from quick chat replies to long-horizon coding tasks

Open weights means fine-tuning, auditing, and on-prem deployment are all on the table
